body:
Crypto markets closed Tuesday in defensive mode with the total market cap slipping 1.24% to $2.58T as extreme fear conditions persisted for the second consecutive session. The Fear & Greed index held steady at 23, up marginally from yesterday's 21, but traders remained skittish amid a cocktail of macro headwinds and security concerns. Volume stayed elevated at $121.9B, suggesting institutional participation even as retail sentiment soured. Bitcoin dominance ticked higher to 57.3% as money rotated into the relative safety of the flagship asset, while altcoins broadly underperformed.

Bitcoin held its ground better than expected, closing at $73,950 with a modest 0.85% decline after testing support around $73,500 during Asian hours. The king coin continues to respect the $74,000 psychological level as resistance, having failed to reclaim it convincingly over the past week despite multiple attempts. Weekly performance remains solid at +3.12%, indicating underlying strength even as short-term momentum wanes. Key levels to monitor are $73,200 support and the $74,800 resistance that's capped rallies since early April. A break below $73,000 could trigger stop-losses and open the door to $71,500.

Ethereum struggled more visibly, dropping 2.35% to $2,322.81 and underperforming Bitcoin by a significant margin. The ETH-BTC ratio showed signs of bottoming from 2026 lows according to CoinDesk reporting, though today's action suggests any recovery remains fragile. Solana bore the brunt of altcoin selling pressure with a 3.43% decline to $83.05, while Polkadot extended its weekly slide to 12.57% and Cardano fell 8.25% over 7 days. The broad altcoin weakness reflects risk-off sentiment, with even defensive plays like Chainlink and Uniswap posting modest losses. AVAX showed relative resilience with just a 0.64% decline.

Market anxiety stems from multiple vectors converging simultaneously. Security breaches dominated headlines with North Korean hackers deploying AI-enabled social engineering in the Zerion attack, while Apple removed a fake Ledger app that stole $9.5M from investors. These incidents amplify existing concerns about crypto infrastructure safety. On the regulatory front, SocGen's launch of a MiCA-compliant stablecoin signals institutional adoption progress, but Japan's Rakuten integrating XRP for payments provides a rare bright spot. Bitmine's staggering $3.8B Q1 loss filing adds to institutional uncertainty, while Bitcoin developers' work on quantum defenses raises questions about long-term protocol stability.

The week ahead hinges on whether Bitcoin can reclaim $74,000 decisively and drag altcoins higher, or if continued macro pressure forces a test of deeper support levels. Ethereum's performance relative to Bitcoin will be crucial for broader altcoin sentiment, with the ETH-BTC ratio sitting at critical technical levels. Any escalation in security incidents could trigger another wave of selling, while positive regulatory developments might provide relief. Watch for institutional flows and whether the extreme fear reading can reverse without a significant fundamental catalyst.
